LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CHARLES KING'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION & STAY COURT PROCEEDINGS. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: MOTIONS TO COMPEL ARBITRATION BY DRAGONI, TALLEY, PETROSKI, KING, EIFE, BARBER: DENIED. THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT NAMES PARTIES WHO ARE NOT PARTIES TO THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT AND ALLEGES A COMMON COURSE OF CONDUCT THAT HAS AS ITS CENTRAL ALLEGATION THE MISAPPROPRIATION OF PLAINTIFF'S TRADE SECRETS AND ACTS OF UNFAIR COMPETITION. THE EMPLOYMENT AGREEMENT AT PARAGRAPH 7 EXCLUDES INJUNCTIVE AND EQUITABLE RELIEF FOR SUCH CONDUCT FROM THE AGREEMENT TO ARBITRATE. PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 1281.2(C), THE COURT FINDS THAT CONFLICTING RULINGS MAY WELL RESULT IF THE REQUESTED ARBITRATION PROCEEDS. ALL OF THE RELEVANT PARTIES ARE INVOLVED IN THE LITIGATION, BOTH THOSE SUBJECT TO THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT AND THOSE WHO ARE NOT. THE PENDING LITIGATION PROVIDES THE OPTIMAL FORUM TO RESOLVE THIS DISPUTE. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF WOULD BE SIGNIFICANTLY IMPACTED IF THIS ACTION WAS STAYED PENDING ARBITRATION. FOR THESE REASONS, THE COURT DENIES THE MOTION AND STAYS THE REQUEST TO ARBITRATE PENDING THE OUTCOME OF THE COURT ACTION. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

Ntc Of Mtn & Mtn To Compel Arbitration & Stay Court Proceedings; Petition,
Off Calendar Mar-05-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CHARLES KING'S MOTION To Compel Arbitration & Stay Court Proceedings.
NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: Motions to compel arbitration by Dragoni, Talley, Petroski, King, Eife, Barber: Denied. The First Amended Complaint names parties who are not parties to the arbitration agreement and alleges a common course of conduct that has as its central allegation the misappropriation of plaintiff's trade secrets and acts of unfair competition. The employment agreement at paragraph 7 excludes injunctive and equitable relief for such conduct from the agreement to arbitrate. Pursuant to CCP section 1281.2(c), the Court finds that conflicting rulings may well result if the requested arbitration proceeds. All of the relevant parties are involved in the litigation, both those subject to the arbitration agreement and those who are not. The pending litigation provides the optimal forum to resolve this dispute. Plaintiff's request for injunctive relief would be significantly impacted if this action was stayed pending arbitration. For these reasons, the Court denies the motion and stays the request to arbitrate pending the outcome of the court action. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: Motions to quash by Talley, Barber, King and Petroski: Denied. A general appearance was made on behalf of the "defendants" when counsel submitted two case management statements. Mansour v. Superior Court (1995) 38 Cal.App.4th 1750, 1756. Consistent with this appearance is counsel's representation to the Court at a law and motion hearing that counsel represented the other "defendants". Further, the employment contract calls for any arbitration to occur in California. These defendants have sought to enforce that order and by doing so, have made it clear that they are prepared to submit to the jurisdiction of this Court for any arbitration award would be enforced by this Court. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Ntc Of Mtn & Mtn To Compel Arbitration & Stay Court Proceedings, Petition,
Off Calendar Mar-05-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT RANDY BARBER'S MOTION To Compel Arbitration & Stay Court Proceedings.
NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: Motions to compel arbitration by Dragoni, Talley, Petroski, King, Eife, Barber: Denied. The First Amended Complaint names parties who are not parties to the arbitration agreement and alleges a common course of conduct that has as its central allegation the misappropriation of plaintiff's trade secrets and acts of unfair competition. The employment agreement at paragraph 7 excludes injunctive and equitable relief for such conduct from the agreement to arbitrate. Pursuant to CCP section 1281.2(c), the Court finds that conflicting rulings may well result if the requested arbitration proceeds. All of the relevant parties are involved in the litigation, both those subject to the arbitration agreement and those who are not. The pending litigation provides the optimal forum to resolve this dispute. Plaintiff's request for injunctive relief would be significantly impacted if this action was stayed pending arbitration. For these reasons, the Court denies the motion and stays the request to arbitrate pending the outcome of the court action. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
